translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I composed the first discourse, O Theophilus, concerning all things which Jesus began both to do and to teach,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: until that day in which, having by the Holy Spirit charged the apostles whom he had chosen, he was taken up;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to whom also he presented himself living, after he had suffered, with many proofs; being seen by them during forty days, and speaking of the things which concern the kingdom ofGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and, being assembled with [them], commanded them not to depart from Jerusalem, but to await the promise of the Father, which [said he] ye have heard of me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For John indeed baptised with water, but ye shall be baptised with the Holy Spirit after now not many days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They therefore, being come together, asked him saying, Lord, is it at this time that thou restorest the kingdom to Israel?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, It is not yours to know times or seasons, which the Father has placed in his own authority;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but ye will receive power, the Holy Spirit having come upon you, and ye shall be my witnesses both in Jerusalem, and in all Judaea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having said these things he was taken up, they beholding [him], and a cloud received him out of their sight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they were gazing into heaven, as he was going, behold, also two men stood by them in white clothing,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who also said, Men of Galilee, why do ye stand looking into heaven? This Jesus who has been taken up from you into heaven, shall thus come in the manner in which ye have beheld him going into heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then they returned to Jerusalem from the mount called [the mount] of Olives, which is near Jerusalem, a sabbath-day's journey off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they were come into [the city], they went up to the upper chamber, where were staying both Peter, and John, and James, and Andrew, Philip and Thomas, Bartholomew and Matthew, James [son] of Alphaeus, and Simon the zealot, and Jude [the brother] of James.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These gave themselves all with one accord to continual prayer, with [several] women, and Mary the mother of Jesus, and with his brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in those days Peter, standing up in the midst of the brethren, said, (the crowd of names [who were] together [was] about a hundred and twenty,)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Brethren, it was necessary that the scripture should have been fulfilled, which the Holy Spirit spoke before, by the mouth of David, concerning Judas, who became guide to those who took Jesus;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for he was numbered amongst us, and had received a part in this service.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (This [man] then indeed got a field with [the] reward of iniquity, and, having fallen down headlong, burst in the midst, and all his bowels gushed out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was known to all the inhabitants of Jerusalem, so that that field was called in their own dialect Aceldama; that is, field of blood.)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it is written in [the] book of Psalms, Let his homestead become desolate, and let there be no dweller in it; and, Let another take his overseership.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is necessary therefore, that of the men who have assembled with us all [the] time in which the Lord Jesus came in and went out among us,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: beginning from the baptism of John until the day in which he was taken up from us, one of these should be a witness with us of his resurrection.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they appointed two, Joseph, who was called Barsabas, who had been surnamed Justus, and Matthias.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they prayed, and said, Thou Lord, knower of the hearts of all, shew which one of these two thou hast chosen,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to receive the lot of this service and apostleship, from which Judas transgressing fell to go to his own place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they gave lots on them, and the lot fell on Matthias, and he was numbered with the eleven apostles.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the day of Pentecost was now accomplishing, they were all together in one place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there came suddenly a sound out of heaven as of a violent impetuous blowing, and filled all the house where they were sitting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there appeared to them parted tongues, as of fire, and it sat upon each one of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were all filled with [the] Holy Spirit, and began to speak with other tongues as the Spirit gave to them to speak forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now there were dwelling at Jerusalem Jews, pious men, from every nation of those under heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the rumour of this having spread, the multitude came together and were confounded, because each one heard them speaking in his own dialect.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all were amazed and wondered, saying, Behold, are not all these who are speaking Galilaeans?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and how do we hear [them] each in our own dialect in which we have been born,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Parthians, and Medes, and Elamites, and those who inhabit Mesopotamia, and Judaea, and Cappadocia, Pontus and Asia,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: both Phrygia and Pamphylia, Egypt, and the parts of Libya which adjoin Cyrene, and the Romans sojourning [here], both Jews and proselytes,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Cretans and Arabians, we hear them speaking in our own tongues the great things ofGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were all amazed and in perplexity, saying one to another, What would this mean?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But others mocking said, They are full of new wine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Peter, standing up with the eleven, lifted up his voice and spoke forth to them, Men of Judaea, and all ye inhabitants of Jerusalem, let this be known to you, and give heed to my words:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for these are not full of wine, as ye suppose, for it is the third hour of the day;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but this is that which was spoken through the prophet Joel,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be in the last days, saithGod, [that] I will pour out of my Spirit upon all flesh; and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, and your young men shall see visions, and your elders shall dream with dreams;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: yea, even upon my bondmen and upon my bondwomen in those days will I pour out of my Spirit, and they shall prophesy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will give wonders in the heaven above and signs on the earth below, blood, and fire, and vapour of smoke:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the sun shall be changed to darkness and the moon to blood, before the great and gloriously appearing day of [the] Lord come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be that whosoever shall call upon the name of [the] Lord shall be saved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Men of Israel, hear these words: Jesus the Nazaraean, a man borne witness to byGod to you by works of power and wonders and signs, whichGod wrought by him in your midst, as yourselves know

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — him, given up by the determinate counsel and foreknowledge ofGod, ye, by [the] hand of lawless [men], have crucified and slain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: WhomGod has raised up, having loosed the pains of death, inasmuch as it was not possible that he should be held by its power;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for David says as to him, I foresaw the Lord continually before me, because he is at my right hand that I may not be moved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore has my heart rejoiced and my tongue exulted; yea more, my flesh also shall dwell in hope,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for thou wilt not leave my soul in hades, nor wilt thou give thy gracious one to see corruption.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast made known to me [the] paths of life, thou wilt fill me with joy with thy countenance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Brethren, let it be allowed to speak with freedom to you concerning the patriarch David, that he has both died and been buried, and his monument is amongst us unto this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Being therefore a prophet, and knowing thatGod had sworn to him with an oath, of the fruit of his loins to set upon his throne;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he, seeing [it] before, spoke concerning the resurrection of the Christ, that neither has he been left in hades nor his flesh seen corruption.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This Jesus hasGod raised up, whereof all we are witnesses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having therefore been exalted by the right hand ofGod, and having received of the Father the promise of the Holy Spirit, he has poured out this which ye behold and hear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For David has not ascended into the heavens, but he says himself, The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit at my right hand

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: until I have put thine enemies [to be] the footstool of thy feet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the whole house of Israel therefore know assuredly thatGod has made him, this Jesus whom ye have crucified, both Lord and Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having heard [it] they were pricked in heart, and said to Peter and the other apostles, What shall we do, brethren?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter said to them, Repent, and be baptised, each one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ, for remission of sins, and ye will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For to you is the promise and to your children, and to all who [are] afar off, as many as [the] Lord ourGod may call.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And with many other words he testified and exhorted them, saying, Be saved from this perverse generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Those then who had accepted his word were baptised; and there were added in that day about three thousand souls.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they persevered in the teaching and fellowship of the apostles, in breaking of bread and prayers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And fear was upon every soul, and many wonders and signs took place through the apostles' means.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all that believed were together, and had all things common,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and sold their possessions and substance, and distributed them to all, according as any one might have need.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And every day, being constantly in the temple with one accord, and breaking bread in [the] house, they received their food with gladness and simplicity of heart,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: praisingGod, and having favour with all the people; and the Lord added [to the assembly] daily those that were to be saved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter and John went up together into the temple at the hour of prayer, [which is] the ninth [hour];

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and a certain man who was lame from his mother's womb was being carried, whom they placed every day at the gate of the temple called Beautiful, to ask alms of those who were going into the temple;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who, seeing Peter and John about to enter into the temple, asked to receive alms.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter, looking stedfastly upon him with John, said, Look on us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he gave heed to them, expecting to receive something from them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Peter said, Silver and gold I have not; but what I have, this give I to thee: In the name of Jesus Christ the Nazaraean rise up and walk.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having taken hold of him [by] the right hand he raised him up, and immediately his feet and ankle bones were made strong.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And leaping up he stood and walked, and entered with them into the temple, walking, and leaping, and praisingGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the people saw him walking and praisingGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they recognised him, that it was he who sat for alms at the Beautiful gate of the temple; and they were filled with wonder and amazement at what had happened to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he held Peter and John, all the people ran together to them in the portico which is called Solomon's, greatly wondering.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter, seeing it, answered the people, Men of Israel, why are ye astonished at this? or why do ye gaze on us as if we had by our own power or piety made him to walk?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: TheGod of Abraham and Isaac and Jacob, theGod of our fathers, has glorified his servant Jesus, whom ye delivered up, and denied him in the presence of Pilate, when he had judged that he should be let go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ye denied the holy and righteous one, and asked that a man [that was] a murderer should be granted to you;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but the originator of life ye slew, whomGod raised from among [the] dead, whereof we are witnesses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, by faith in his name, his name has made this [man] strong whom ye behold and know; and the faith which is by him has given him this complete soundness in the presence of you all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, brethren, I know that ye did it in ignorance, as also your rulers;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: butGod has thus fulfilled what he had announced beforehand by the mouth of all the prophets, that his Christ should suffer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Repent therefore and be converted, for the blotting out of your sins, so that times of refreshing may come from [the] presence of the Lord,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he may send Jesus Christ, who was foreordained for you,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: whom heaven indeed must receive till [the] times of [the] restoring of all things, of whichGod has spoken by the mouth of his holy prophets since time began.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moses indeed said, A prophet shall [the] Lord yourGod raise up to you out of your brethren like me: him shall ye hear in everything whatsoever he shall say to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be that whatsoever soul shall not hear that prophet shall be destroyed from among the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And indeed all the prophets from Samuel and those in succession after [him], as many as have spoken, have announced also these days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye are the sons of the prophets and of the covenant whichGod appointed to our fathers, saying to Abraham, And in thy seed shall all the families of the earth be blessed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To you firstGod, having raised up his servant, has sent him, blessing you in turning each one [of you] from your wickedness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they were speaking to the people, the priests and captain of the temple and the Sadducees came upon them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: being distressed on account of their teaching the people and preaching by Jesus the resurrection from among [the] dead;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they laid hands on them, and put them in ward till the morrow; for it was already evening.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But many of those who had heard the word believed; and the number of the men had become [about] five thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass on the morrow that their rulers and elders and scribes were gathered together at Jerusalem,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Annas the high priest, and Caiaphas, and John, and Alexander, and as many as were of [the] high priestly family;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having placed them in the midst they inquired, In what power or in what name have ye done this?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Peter, filled with [the] Holy Spirit, said to them, Rulers of the people and elders [of Israel],

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: if we this day are called upon to answer as to the good deed [done] to the infirm man, how he has been healed,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: be it known to you all, and to all the people of Israel, that in the name of Jesus Christ the Nazaraean, whom ye have crucified, whomGod has raised from among [the] dead, by him this [man] stands here before you sound [in body].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He is the stone which has been set at nought by you the builders, which is become the corner stone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And salvation is in none other, for neither is there another name under heaven which is given among men by which we must be saved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But seeing the boldness of Peter and John, and perceiving that they were unlettered and uninstructed men, they wondered; and they recognised them that they were with Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And beholding the man who had been healed standing with them, they had nothing to reply;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but having commanded them to go out of the council they conferred with one another,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, What shall we do to these men? for that indeed an evident sign has come to pass through their means is manifest to all that inhabit Jerusalem, and we cannot deny it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But that it be not further spread among the people, let us threaten them severely no longer to speak to any man in this name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having called them, they charged [them] not to speak at all nor teach in the name of Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Peter and John answering said to them, If it be righteous beforeGod to listen to you rather than toGod, judge ye;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for as for us we cannot refrain from speaking of the things which we have seen and heard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they, having further threatened them, let them go, finding no way how they might punish them, on account of the people, because all glorifiedGod for what had taken place;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for the man on whom this sign of healing had taken place was above forty years old.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having been let go, they came to their own [company], and reported all that the chief priests and elders had said to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they, having heard [it], lifted up [their] voice with one accord toGod, and said, Lord, thou art theGod who made the heaven and the earth and the sea, and all that is in them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who hast said by the mouth of thy servant David, Why have [the] nations raged haughtily and [the] peoples meditated vain things?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The kings of the earth were there, and the rulers were gathered together against the Lord and against his Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For in truth against thy holy servant Jesus, whom thou hadst anointed, both Herod and Pontius Pilate, with [the] nations, and peoples of Israel, have been gathered together in this city

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to do whatever thy hand and thy counsel had determined before should come to pass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, Lord, look upon their threatenings, and give to thy bondmen with all boldness to speak thy word,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in that thou stretchest out thy hand to heal, and that signs and wonders take place through the name of thy holy servant Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they had prayed, the place in which they were assembled shook, and they were all filled with the Holy Spirit, and spoke the word ofGod with boldness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the heart and soul of the multitude of those that had believed were one, and not one said that anything of what he possessed was his own, but all things were common to them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and with great power did the apostles give witness of the resurrection of the Lord Jesus, and great grace was upon them all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For neither was there any one in want among them; for as many as were owners of lands or houses, selling them, brought the price of what was sold

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and laid it at the feet of the apostles; and distribution was made to each according as any one might have need.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph, who had been surnamed Barnabas by the apostles (which is, being interpreted, Son of consolation), a Levite, Cyprian by birth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: being possessed of land, having sold [it], brought the money and laid it at the feet of the apostles.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But a certain man, Ananias by name, with Sapphira his wife, sold a possession,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and put aside for himself part of the price, [his] wife also being privy to it; and having brought a certain part, laid it at the feet of the apostles.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Peter said, Ananias, why has Satan filled thy heart that thou shouldest lie to the Holy Spirit, and put aside for thyself a part of the price of the estate?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: While it remained did it not remain to thee? and sold, was [it not] in thine own power? Why is it that thou hast purposed this thing in thine heart? Thou hast not lied to men, but toGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ananias, hearing these words, fell down and expired. And great fear came upon all who heard [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the young men, rising up, swathed him up for burial, and having carried him out, buried him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass about three hours afterwards, that his wife, not knowing what had happened, came in.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter answered her, Tell me if ye gave the estate for so much? And she said, Yes, for so much.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter said to her, Why [is it] that ye have agreed together to tempt the Spirit of [the] Lord? Lo, the feet of those that have buried thy husband [are] at the door, and they shall carry thee out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she fell down immediately at his feet and expired. And when the young men came in they found her dead; and, having carried her out, they buried her by her husband.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And great fear came upon all the assembly, and upon all who heard these things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And by the hands of the apostles were many signs and wonders done among the people; (and they were all with one accord in Solomon's porch,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but of the rest durst no man join them, but the people magnified them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and believers were more than ever added to the Lord, multitudes both of men and women;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so that they brought out the sick into the streets and put [them] on beds and couches, that at least the shadow of Peter, when he came, might overshadow some one of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the multitude also of the cities round about came together to Jerusalem, bringing sick persons and persons beset by unclean spirits, who were all healed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the high priest rising up, and all they that were with him, which is the sect of the Sadducees, were filled with wrath,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and laid hands on the apostles and put them in the public prison.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But an angel of [the] Lord during the night opened the doors of the prison, and leading them out, said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go ye and stand and speak in the temple to the people all the words of this life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they heard it, they entered very early into the temple and taught. And when the high priest was come, and they that were with him, they called together the council and all the elderhood of the sons of Israel, and sent to the prison to have them brought.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the officers were come, they did not find them in the prison; and returned and reported

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, We found the prison shut with all security, and the keepers standing at the doors; but when we had opened [them], within we found no one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they heard these words, both the priest and the captain of the temple and the chief priests were in perplexity as to them, what this would come to.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And some one coming reported to them, Lo, the men whom ye put in the prison are in the temple, standing and teaching the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the captain, having gone with the officers, brought them, not with violence, for they feared the people, lest they should be stoned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they bring them and set them in the council. And the high priest asked them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, We strictly enjoined you not to teach in this name: and lo, ye have filled Jerusalem with your doctrine, and purpose to bring upon us the blood of this man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Peter answering, and the apostles, said,God must be obeyed rather than men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: TheGod of our fathers has raised up Jesus, whom ye have slain, having hanged on a cross.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Him hasGod exalted by his right hand as leader and saviour, to give repentance to Israel and remission of sins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we are [his] witnesses of these things, and the Holy Spirit also, whichGod has given to those that obey him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they, when they heard [these things], were cut to the heart, and took counsel to kill them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But a certain [man], a Pharisee, named Gamaliel, a teacher of the law, held in honour of all the people, rose up in the council, and commanded to put the men out for a short while,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said to them, Men of Israel, take heed to yourselves as regards these men what ye are going to do;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for before these days Theudas rose up, alleging himself to be somebody, to whom a number of men, about four hundred, were joined; who was slain, and all, as many as obeyed him, were dispersed and came to nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: After him rose Judas the Galilean in the days of the census, and drew away [a number of] people after him; and he perished, and all, as many as obeyed him, were scattered abroad.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now I say to you, Withdraw from these men and let them alone, for if this counsel or this work have its origin from men, it will be destroyed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but if it be fromGod, ye will not be able to put them down, lest ye be found also fighters againstGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they listened to his advice; and having called the apostles, they beat them, and enjoined them not to speak in the name of Jesus, and dismissed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They therefore went their way from [the] presence of the council, rejoicing that they were counted worthy to be dishonoured for the name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And every day, in the temple and in the houses, they ceased not teaching and announcing the glad tidings that Jesus [was] the Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But in those days, the disciples multiplying in number, there arose a murmuring of the Hellenists against the Hebrews because their widows were overlooked in the daily ministration.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the twelve, having called the multitude of the disciples to [them], said, It is not right that we, leaving the word ofGod, should serve tables.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Look out therefore, brethren, from among yourselves seven men, well reported of, full of [the] [Holy] Spirit and wisdom, whom we will establish over this business:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but we will give ourselves up to prayer and the ministry of the word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the saying pleased the whole multitude: and they chose Stephen, a man full of faith and [the] Holy Spirit, and Philip, and Prochorus, and Nicanor, and Timon, and Parmenas, and Nicolas, a proselyte of Antioch,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: whom they set before the apostles; and, having prayed, they laid their hands on them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word ofGod increased; and the number of the disciples in Jerusalem was very greatly multiplied, and a great crowd of the priests obeyed the faith.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Stephen, full of grace and power, wrought wonders and great signs among the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there arose up certain of those of the synagogue called of freedmen, and of Cyrenians, and of Alexandrians, and of those of Cilicia and Asia, disputing with Stephen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were not able to resist the wisdom and the Spirit with which he spoke.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then they suborned men, saying, We have heard him speaking blasphemous words against Moses andGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they roused the people, and the elders, and the scribes. And coming upon [him] they seized him and brought [him] to the council.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they set false witnesses, saying, This man does not cease speaking words against the holy place and the law;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for we have heard him saying, This Jesus the Nazaraean shall destroy this place, and change the customs which Moses taught us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all who sat in the council, looking fixedly on him, saw his face as [the] face of an angel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the high priest said, Are these things then so?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Brethren and fathers, hearken. TheGod of glory appeared to our father Abraham when he was in Mesopotamia, before he dwelt in Charran,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said to him, Go out of thy land and out of thy kindred, and come into the land which I will shew thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then going out of the land of the Chaldeans he dwelt in Charran, and thence, after his father died, he removed him into this land in which ye now dwell.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he did not give him an inheritance in it, not even what his foot could stand on; and promised to give it to him for a possession, and to his seed after him, when he had no child.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod spoke thus: His seed shall be a sojourner in a strange land, and they shall enslave them and evil entreat [them] four hundred years;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the nation to which they shall be in bondage will I judge, saidGod; and after these things they shall come forth and serve me in this place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he gave to him [the] covenant of circumcision; and thus he begat Isaac and circumcised him the eighth day; and Isaac Jacob, and Jacob the twelve patriarchs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the patriarchs, envying Joseph, sold him away into Egypt. AndGod was with him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and delivered him out of all his tribulations, and gave him favour and wisdom in the sight of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and he appointed him chief over Egypt and all his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But a famine came upon all the land of Egypt and Canaan, and great distress, and our fathers found no food.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jacob, having heard of there being corn in Egypt, sent out our fathers first;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the second time Joseph was made known to his brethren, and the family of Joseph became known to Pharaoh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph sent and called down to him his father Jacob and all [his] kindred, seventy-five souls.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jacob went down into Egypt and died, he and our fathers,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and were carried over to Sychem and placed in the sepulchre which Abraham bought for a sum of money of the sons of Emmor the [father] of Sychem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as the time of promise drew near whichGod had promised to Abraham, the people increased and multiplied in Egypt,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: until another king over Egypt arose who did not know Joseph.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He dealt subtilly with our race, and evil entreated the fathers, casting out their infants that they might not live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In which time Moses was born, and was exceedingly lovely, who was nourished three months in the house of his father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he was cast out, the daughter of Pharaoh took him up, and brought him up for herself [to be] for a son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses was instructed in all [the] wisdom of the Egyptians, and he was mighty in his words and deeds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when a period of forty years was fulfilled to him, it came into his heart to look upon his brethren, the sons of Israel;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and seeing a certain one wronged, he defended [him], and avenged him that was being oppressed, smiting the Egyptian.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he thought that his brethren would understand thatGod by his hand was giving them deliverance. But they understood not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the morrow he shewed himself to them as they were contending, and compelled them to peace, saying, Ye are brethren, why do ye wrong one another?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he that was wronging his neighbour thrust him away, saying, Who established thee ruler and judge over us?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dost thou wish to kill me as thou killedst the Egyptian yesterday?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses fled at this saying, and became a sojourner in the land of Madiam, where he begat two sons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when forty years were fulfilled, an angel appeared to him in the wilderness of mount Sinai, in a flame of fire of a bush.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses seeing it wondered at the vision; and as he went up to consider it, there was a voice of [the] Lord,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am theGod of thy fathers, theGod of Abraham, and of Isaac, and of Jacob. And Moses trembled, and durst not consider [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Lord said to him, Loose the sandal of thy feet, for the place on which thou standest is holy ground.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have surely seen the ill treatment of my people which is in Egypt, and I have heard their groan, and have come down to take them out of it; and now, come, I will send thee to Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This Moses, whom they refused, saying, Who made thee ruler and judge? him didGod send [to be] a ruler and deliverer with the hand of the angel who appeared to him in the bush.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He led them out, having wrought wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, and in the Red sea, and in the wilderness forty years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the Moses who said to the sons of Israel, A prophet shallGod raise up to you out of your brethren like me [him shall ye hear].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is he who was in the assembly in the wilderness, with the angel who spoke to him in the mount Sinai, and with our fathers; who received living oracles to give to us;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to whom our fathers would not be subject, but thrust [him] from them, and in their hearts turned back to Egypt,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying to Aaron, Make usgods who shall go before us; for this Moses, who brought us out of the land of Egypt, we know not what has happened to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they made a calf in those days, and offered sacrifice to the idol, and rejoiced in the works of their own hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ButGod turned and delivered them up to serve the host of heaven; as it is written in [the] book of the prophets, Have ye offered me victims and sacrifices forty years in the wilderness, O house of Israel?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, ye took up the tent of Moloch, and the star of [your]god Remphan, the forms which ye made to do homage to them; and I will transport you beyond Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Our fathers had the tent of the testimony in the wilderness, as he that spoke to Moses commanded to make it according to the model which he had seen;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which also our fathers, receiving from their predecessors, brought in with Joshua when they entered into possession of [the lands of] the nations, whomGod drove out from [the] face of our fathers, until the days of David;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who found favour beforeGod, and asked to find a tabernacle for theGod of Jacob;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but Solomon built him a house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the Most High dwells not in [places] made with hands; as says the prophet,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The heaven [is] my throne and the earth the footstool of my feet: what house will ye build me? saith [the] Lord, or where [is the] place of my rest?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: has not my hand made all these things?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: O stiffnecked and uncircumcised in heart and ears, ye do always resist the Holy Spirit; as your fathers, ye also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Which of the prophets have not your fathers persecuted? and they have slain those who announced beforehand concerning the coming of the Just One, of whom ye have now become deliverers up and murderers!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who have received the law as ordained by [the] ministry of angels, and have not kept [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And hearing these things they were cut to the heart, and gnashed their teeth against him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But being full of [the] Holy Spirit, having fixed his eyes on heaven, he saw [the] glory ofGod, and Jesus standing at the right hand ofGod,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said, Lo, I behold the heavens opened, and the Son of man standing at the right hand ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they cried out with a loud voice, and held their ears, and rushed upon him with one accord;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having cast [him] out of the city, they stoned [him]. And the witnesses laid aside their clothes at the feet of a young man called Saul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they stoned Stephen, praying, and saying, Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And kneeling down, he cried with a loud voice, Lord, lay not this sin to their charge. And having said this, he fell asleep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul was consenting to his being killed. And on that day there arose a great persecution against the assembly which was in Jerusalem, and all were scattered into the countries of Judaea and Samaria except the apostles.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And pious men buried Stephen and made great lamentation over him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Saul ravaged the assembly, entering into the houses one after another, and dragging off both men and women delivered them up to prison.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Those then that had been scattered went through [the countries] announcing the glad tidings of the word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Philip, going down to a city of Samaria, preached the Christ to them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the crowds with one accord gave heed to the things spoken by Philip, when they heard [him] and saw the signs which he wrought.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For from many who had unclean spirits they went out, crying with a loud voice; and many that were paralysed and lame were healed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was great joy in that city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But a certain man, by name Simon, had been before in the city, using magic arts, and astonishing the nation of Samaria, saying that himself was some great one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To whom they had all given heed, from small to great, saying, This is the power ofGod which is called great.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they gave heed to him, because that for a long time he had astonished them by his magic arts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when they believed Philip announcing the glad tidings concerning the kingdom ofGod and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptised, both men and women.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Simon also himself believed; and, having been baptised, continued constantly with Philip; and, beholding the signs and great works of power which took place, was astonished.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the apostles who were in Jerusalem, having heard that Samaria had received the word ofGod, sent to them Peter and John;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who, having come down, prayed for them that they might receive [the] Holy Spirit;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for he was not yet fallen upon any of them, only they were baptised to the name of the Lord Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then they laid their hands upon them, and they received [the] Holy Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Simon, having seen that by the laying on of the hands of the apostles the [Holy] Spirit was given, offered them money,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, Give to me also this power, in order that on whomsoever I may lay hands he may receive [the] Holy Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter said to him, Thy money go with thee to destruction, because thou hast thought that the gift ofGod can be obtained by money.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast neither part nor lot in this matter, for thy heart is not upright beforeGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Repent therefore of this thy wickedness, and supplicate the Lord, if indeed the thought of thy heart may be forgiven thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for I see thee to be in the gall of bitterness, and bond of unrighteousness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Simon answering said, Supplicate ye for me to the Lord, so that nothing may come upon me of the things of which ye have spoken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They therefore, having testified and spoken the word of the Lord, returned to Jerusalem, and announced the glad tidings to many villages of the Samaritans.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But [the] angel of [the] Lord spoke to Philip, saying, Rise up and go southward on the way which goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza: the same is desert.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he rose up and went. And lo, an Ethiopian, a eunuch, a man in power under Candace queen of the Ethiopians, who was over all her treasure, who had come to worship at Jerusalem,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: was returning and sitting in his chariot: and he was reading the prophet Esaias.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Spirit said to Philip, Approach and join this chariot.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Philip, running up, heard him reading the prophet Esaias, and said, Dost thou then know what thou art reading of?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, How should I then be able unless some one guide me? And he begged Philip to come up and sit with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the passage of the scripture which he read was this: He was led as a sheep to slaughter, and as a lamb is dumb in presence of him that shears him, thus he opens not his mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In his humiliation his judgment has been taken away, and who shall declare his generation? for his life is taken from the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the eunuch answering Philip said, I pray thee, concerning whom does the prophet say this? of himself or of some other?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Philip, opening his mouth and beginning from that scripture, announced the glad tidings of Jesus to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they went along the way, they came upon a certain water, and the eunuch says, Behold water; what hinders my being baptised?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he commanded the chariot to stop. And they went down both to the water, both Philip and the eunuch, and he baptised him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when they came up out of the water [the] Spirit of [the] Lord caught away Philip, and the eunuch saw him no longer, for he went on his way rejoicing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Philip was found at Azotus, and passing through he announced the glad tidings to all the cities till he came to Caesarea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Saul, still breathing out threatenings and slaughter against the disciples of the Lord, came to the high priest

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and asked of him letters to Damascus, to the synagogues, so that if he found any who were of the way, both men and women, he might bring [them] bound to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as he was journeying, it came to pass that he drew near to Damascus; and suddenly there shone round about him a light out of heaven,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and falling on the earth he heard a voice saying to him, Saul, Saul, why dost thou persecute me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Who art thou, Lord? And he [said], I am Jesus, whom thou persecutest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But rise up and enter into the city, and it shall be told thee what thou must do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the men who were travelling with him stood speechless, hearing the voice but beholding no one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Saul rose up from the earth, and his eyes being opened he saw no one. But leading [him] by the hand they brought him into Damascus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he was three days without seeing, and neither ate nor drank.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was a certain disciple in Damascus by name Ananias. And the Lord said to him in a vision, Ananias. And he said, Behold, [here am] I, Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Lord [said] to him, Rise up and go into the street which is called Straight, and seek in the house of Judas one by name Saul, [he is] of Tarsus: for, behold, he is praying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and has seen [in a vision] a man by name Ananias coming in and putting his hand on him, so that he should see.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ananias answered, Lord, I have heard from many concerning this man how much evil he has done to thy saints at Jerusalem;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and here he has authority from the chief priests to bind all who call upon thy name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Lord said to him, Go, for this [man] is an elect vessel to me, to bear my name before both nations and kings and [the] sons of Israel:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for I will shew to him how much he must suffer for my name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ananias went and entered into the house; and laying his hands upon him he said, Saul, brother, the Lord has sent me, Jesus that appeared to thee in the way in which thou camest, that thou mightest see, and be filled with [the] Holy Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And straightway there fell from his eyes as it were scales, and he saw, and rising up was baptised;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and, having received food, got strength. And he was with the disciples who [were] in Damascus certain days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And straightway in the synagogues he preached Jesus that he is the Son ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all who heard were astonished and said, Is not this he who destroyed in Jerusalem those who called on this name, and here was come for this purpose, that he might bring them bound to the chief priests?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Saul increased the more in power, and confounded the Jews who dwelt in Damascus, proving that this is the Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now when many days were fulfilled, the Jews consulted together to kill him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But their plot became known to Saul. And they watched also the gates both day and night, that they might kill him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but the disciples took him by night and let him down through the wall, lowering him in a basket.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having arrived at Jerusalem he essayed to join himself to the disciples, and all were afraid of him, not believing that he was a disciple.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Barnabas took him and brought him to the apostles, and related to them how he had seen the Lord in the way, and that he had spoken to him, and how in Damascus he had spoken boldly in the name of Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he was with them coming in and going out at Jerusalem,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and speaking boldly in the name of the Lord. And he spoke and discussed with the Hellenists; but they sought to kill him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the brethren knowing it, brought him down to Caesarea and sent him away to Tarsus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The assemblies then throughout the whole of Judaea and Galilee and Samaria had peace, being edified and walking in the fear of the Lord, and were increased through the comfort of the Holy Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now it came to pass that Peter, passing through all [quarters], descended also to the saints who inhabited Lydda.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he found there a certain man, Aeneas by name, who had been lying for eight years upon a couch, who was paralysed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter said to him, Aeneas, Jesus, the Christ, heals thee: rise up, and make thy couch for thyself. And straightway he rose up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all who inhabited Lydda and the Saron saw him, who turned to the Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in Joppa there was a certain female disciple, by name Tabitha, which being interpreted means Dorcas. She was full of good works and alms-deeds which she did.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in those days that she grew sick and died; and, having washed her, they put her in [the] upper room.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Lydda being near to Joppa, the disciples having heard that Peter was there, sent two men to him, beseeching him, Thou must not delay coming to us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter rising up went with them, whom, when arrived, they brought up into the upper chamber; and all the widows stood by him weeping and shewing him the body-coats and garments which Dorcas had made while she was with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Peter, putting them all out, and kneeling down, prayed. And, turning to the body, he said, Tabitha, arise. And she opened her eyes, and, seeing Peter, sat up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having given her [his] hand, he raised her up, and having called the saints and the widows, presented her living.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it became known throughout the whole of Joppa, and many believed on the Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass that he remained many days in Joppa with a certain Simon, a tanner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But a certain man in Caesarea, — by name Cornelius, a centurion of the band called Italic,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: pious, and fearingGod with all his house, [both] giving much alms to the people, and supplicatingGod continually,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — saw plainly in a vision, about the ninth hour of the day, an angel ofGod coming unto him, and saying to him, Cornelius.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he, having fixed his eyes upon him, and become full of fear, said, What is it, Lord? And he said to him, Thy prayers and thine alms have gone up for a memorial beforeGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now send men to Joppa and fetch Simon, who is surnamed Peter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He lodges with a certain Simon, a tanner, whose house is by the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the angel who was speaking to him had departed, having called two of his household and a pious soldier of those who were constantly with him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and related all things to them, he sent them to Joppa.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the morrow, as these were journeying and drawing near to the city, Peter went up on the house to pray, about the sixth hour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he became hungry and desired to eat. But as they were making ready an ecstasy came upon him:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he beholds the heaven opened, and a certain vessel descending, as a great sheet, [bound] by [the] four corners [and] let down to the earth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in which were all the quadrupeds and creeping things of the earth, and the fowls of the heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was a voice to him, Rise, Peter, slay and eat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter said, In no wise, Lord; for I have never eaten anything common or unclean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [there was] a voice again the second time to him, WhatGod has cleansed, do not thou make common.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this took place thrice, and the vessel was straightway taken up into heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as Peter doubted in himself what the vision which he had seen might mean, behold also the men who were sent by Cornelius, having sought out the house of Simon, stood at the gate,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having called [some one], they inquired if Simon who was surnamed Peter was lodged there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as Peter continued pondering over the vision, the Spirit said to him, Behold, three men seek thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but rise up, go down, and go with them, nothing doubting, because I have sent them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter going down to the men said, Behold, I am he whom ye seek: what is the cause for which ye come?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, Cornelius, a centurion, a righteous man, and fearingGod, and borne witness to by the whole nation of the Jews, has been divinely instructed by a holy angel to send for thee to his house, and hear words from thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having therefore invited them in, he lodged them. And on the morrow, rising up he went away with them, and certain of the brethren from Joppa went with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the morrow they came to Caesarea. But Cornelius was looking for them, having called together his kinsmen and [his] intimate friends.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Peter was now coming in, Cornelius met him, and falling down did [him] homage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Peter made him rise, saying, Rise up: I myself also am a man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went in, talking with him, and found many gathered together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Ye know how it is unlawful for a Jew to be joined or come to one of a strange race, and to meGod has shewn to call no man common or unclean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore also, having been sent for, I came without saying anything against it. I inquire therefore for what reason ye have sent for me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Cornelius said, Four days ago I had been [fasting] unto this hour, and the ninth [I was] praying in my house, and lo, a man stood before me in bright clothing,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said, Cornelius, thy prayer has been heard, and thy alms have come in remembrance beforeGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Send therefore to Joppa and fetch Simon, who is surnamed Peter; he lodges in the house of Simon, a tanner, by the sea [who when he is come will speak to thee].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Immediately therefore I sent to thee, and thou hast well done in coming. Now therefore we are all present beforeGod to hear all things that are commanded thee ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter opening his mouth said, Of a truth I perceive thatGod is no respecter of persons,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but in every nation he that fears him and works righteousness is acceptable to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The word which he sent to the sons of Israel, preaching peace by Jesus Christ, (he is Lord of all things,)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ye know; the testimony which has spread through the whole of Judaea, beginning from Galilee after the baptism which John preached —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus who [was] of Nazareth: howGod anointed him with [the] Holy Spirit and with power; who went through [all quarters] doing good, and healing all that were under the power of the devil, becauseGod was with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We also [are] witnesses of all things which he did both in the country of the Jews and in Jerusalem; whom they also slew, having hanged him on a cross.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This [man]God raised up the third day and gave him to be openly seen,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: not of all the people, but of witnesses who were chosen before ofGod, us who have eaten and drunk with him after he arose from among [the] dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he commanded us to preach to the people, and to testify that he it is who was determinately appointed ofGod [to be] judge of living and dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To him all the prophets bear witness that every one that believes on him will receive through his name remission of sins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: While Peter was yet speaking these words the Holy Spirit fell upon all those who were hearing the word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the faithful of the circumcision were astonished, as many as came with Peter, that upon the nations also the gift of the Holy Spirit was poured out:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for they heard them speaking with tongues and magnifyingGod. Then Peter answered,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Can any one forbid water that these should not be baptised, who have received the Holy Spirit as we also [did]?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he commanded them to be baptised in the name of the Lord. Then they begged him to stay some days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the apostles and the brethren who were in Judaea heard that the nations also had received the word ofGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and when Peter went up to Jerusalem, they of the circumcision contended with him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, Thou wentest in to men uncircumcised and hast eaten with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Peter began and set forth [the matter] to them in order, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I was in the city of Joppa praying, and in an ecstasy I saw a vision, a certain vessel descending like a great sheet, let down by four corners out of heaven, and it came even to me:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: on which having fixed mine eyes, I considered, and saw the quadrupeds of the earth, and the wild beasts, and the creeping things, and the fowls of the heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I heard also a voice saying to me, Rise up, Peter, slay and eat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said, In no wise, Lord, for common or unclean has never entered into my mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a voice answered the second time out of heaven, WhatGod has cleansed, do not thou make common.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this took place thrice, and again all was drawn up into heaven;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and lo, immediately three men were at the house in which I was, sent to me from Caesarea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Spirit said to me to go with them, nothing doubting. And there went with me these six brethren also, and we entered into the house of the man,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he related to us how he had seen the angel in his house, standing and saying [to him], Send [men] to Joppa and fetch Simon, who is surnamed Peter,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who shall speak words to thee whereby thou shalt be saved, thou and all thy house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell upon them even as upon us also at the beginning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I remembered the word of the Lord, how he said, John baptised with water, but ye shall be baptised with [the] Holy Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thenGod has given them the same gift as also to us when we had believed on the Lord Jesus Christ, who indeed was I to be able to forbidGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they heard these things they held their peace, and glorifiedGod, saying, Then indeedGod has to the nations also granted repentance to life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They then who had been scattered abroad through the tribulation that took place on the occasion of Stephen, passed through [the country] to Phoenicia and Cyprus and Antioch, speaking the word to no one but to Jews alone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But there were certain of them, Cyprians and Cyrenians, who entering into Antioch spoke to the Greeks also, announcing the glad tidings of the Lord Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [the] Lord's hand was with them, and a great number believed and turned to the Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the report concerning them reached the ears of the assembly which was in Jerusalem, and they sent out Barnabas to go through as far as Antioch:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who, having arrived and seeing the grace ofGod, rejoiced, and exhorted all with purpose of heart to abide with the Lord;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for he was a good man and full of [the] Holy Spirit and of faith; and a large crowd [of people] were added to the Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went away to Tarsus to seek out Saul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having found [him], he brought him to Antioch. And so it was with them that for a whole year they were gathered together in the assembly and taught a large crowd: and the disciples were first called Christians in Antioch.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now in these days prophets went down from Jerusalem to Antioch;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and one from among them, by name Agabus, rose up and signified by the Spirit that there was going to be a great famine over all the inhabited earth, which also came to pass under Claudius.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they determined, according as any one of the disciples was well off, each of them to send to the brethren who dwelt in Judaea, to minister [to them];

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which also they did, sending it to the elders by the hand of Barnabas and Saul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At that time Herod the king laid his hands on some of those of the assembly to do them hurt,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and slew James, the brother of John, with the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And seeing that it was pleasing to the Jews, he went on to take Peter also: (and they were the days of unleavened bread:)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: whom having seized he put in prison, having delivered him to four quaternions of soldiers to keep, purposing after the passover to bring him out to the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Peter therefore was kept in the prison; but unceasing prayer was made by the assembly toGod concerning him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Herod was going to bring him forth, that night Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, and guards before the door kept the prison.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And lo, an angel of [the] Lord came there, and a light shone in the prison: and having smitten the side of Peter, he roused him up, saying, Rise up quickly. And his chains fell off his hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the angel said to him, Gird thyself, and bind on thy sandals. And he did so. And he says to him, Cast thine upper garment about thee and follow me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And going forth he followed [him] and did not know that what was happening by means of the angel was real, but supposed he saw a vision.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having passed through a first and second guard, they came to the iron gate which leads into the city, which opened to them of itself; and going forth they went down one street, and immediately the angel left him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter, being come to himself, said, Now I know certainly that [the] Lord has sent forth his angel and has taken me out of the hand of Herod and all the expectation of the people of the Jews.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having become clearly conscious [in himself], he came to the house of Mary, the mother of John who was surnamed Mark, where were many gathered together and praying.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he had knocked at the door of the entry, a maid came to listen, by name Rhoda;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having recognised the voice of Peter, through joy did not open the entry, but running in, reported that Peter was standing before the entry.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to her, Thou art mad. But she maintained that it was so. And they said, It is his angel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Peter continued knocking: and having opened, they saw him and were astonished.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having made a sign to them with his hand to be silent, he related [to them] how the Lord had brought him out of prison; and he said, Report these things to James and to the brethren. And he went out and went to another place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when it was day there was no small disturbance among the soldiers, what then was become of Peter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Herod having sought him and not found him, having examined the guards, commanded [them] to be executed. And he went down from Judaea to Caesarea and stayed [there].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he was in bitter hostility with [the] Tyrians and Sidonians; but they came to him with one accord, and, having gained Blastus the king's chamberlain, sought peace, because their country was nourished by the king's.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on a set day, clothed in royal apparel and sitting on the elevated seat [of honour], Herod made a public oration to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people cried out, Agod's voice and not a man's.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately an angel of [the] Lord smote him, because he did not give the glory toGod, and he expired, eaten of worms.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the word ofGod grew and spread itself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Barnabas and Saul returned from Jerusalem, having fulfilled the service [entrusted to them], taking also with them John, surnamed Mark.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now there were in Antioch, in the assembly which was [there], prophets and teachers: Barnabas, and Simeon who was called Niger, and Lucius the Cyrenian, and Manaen, foster-brother of Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they were ministering to the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, Separate me now Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then, having fasted and prayed, and having laid [their] hands on them, they let [them] go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They therefore, having been sent forth by the Holy Spirit, went down to Seleucia, and thence sailed away to Cyprus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And being in Salamis, they announced the word ofGod in the synagogues of the Jews. And they had John also as [their] attendant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having passed through the whole island as far as Paphos, they found a certain man a magician, a false prophet, a Jew, whose name was Bar-jesus,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who was with the proconsul Sergius Paulus, an intelligent man. He, having called Barnabas and Saul to [him], desired to hear the word ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Elymas the magician (for so his name is by interpretation) opposed them, seeking to turn away the proconsul from the faith.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Saul, who also [is] Paul, filled with [the] Holy Spirit, fixing his eyes upon him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: said, O full of all deceit and all craft: son of [the] devil, enemy of all righteousness; wilt thou not cease perverting the right paths of [the] Lord?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now behold, [the] Lord's hand [is] upon thee, and thou shalt be blind, not seeing the sun for a season. And immediately there fell upon him a mist and darkness; and going about he sought persons who should lead him by the hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the proconsul, seeing what had happened, believed, being amazed at the teaching of the Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having sailed from Paphos, Paul and his company came to Perga of Pamphylia; and John separated from them and returned to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they, passing through from Perga, came to Antioch of Pisidia; and entering into the synagogue on the sabbath day they sat down.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after the reading of the law and the prophets, the rulers of the synagogue sent to them, saying, Brethren, if ye have any word of exhortation to the people, speak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Paul, rising up and making a sign with the hand, said, Israelites, and ye that fearGod, hearken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: TheGod of this people Israel chose our fathers, and exalted the people in their sojourn in [the] land of Egypt, and with a high arm brought them out of it,
