translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and for a time of about forty years he nursed them in the desert.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan, he gave them their land as an inheritance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after these things he gave [them] judges till Samuel the prophet, [to the end of] about four hundred and fifty years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And then they asked for a king, andGod gave to them Saul, son of Kis, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, during forty years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having removed him he raised up to them David for king, of whom also bearing witness he said, I have found David, the son of Jesse, a man after my heart, who shall do all my will.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of this man's seed according to promise hasGod brought to Israel a Saviour, Jesus;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: John having proclaimed before the face of his entry [among the people] [the] baptism of repentance to all the people of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as John was fulfilling his course he said, Whom do ye suppose that I am? I am not [he]. But behold, there comes one after me, the sandal of whose feet I am not worthy to loose.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Brethren, sons of Abraham's race, and those who among you fearGod, to you has the word of this salvation been sent:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for those who dwell in Jerusalem, and their rulers, not having known him, have fulfilled also the voices of the prophets which are read on every sabbath, [by] judging [him].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having found no cause of death [in him], they begged of Pilate that he might be slain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they had fulfilled all things written concerning him, they took him down from the cross and put him in a sepulchre;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: butGod raised him from among [the] dead,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who appeared for many days to those who had come up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who are now his witnesses to the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we declare unto you the glad tidings of the promise made to the fathers,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thatGod has fulfilled this to us their children, having raised up Jesus; as it is also written in the second psalm, Thou art my Son: this day have I begotten thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But that he raised him from among [the] dead, no more to return to corruption, he spoke thus: I will give to you the faithful mercies of David.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore also he says in another, Thou wilt not suffer thy gracious one to see corruption.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For David indeed, having in his own generation ministered to the will ofGod, fell asleep, and was added to his fathers and saw corruption.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he whomGod raised up did not see corruption.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be it known unto you, therefore, brethren, that through this man remission of sins is preached to you,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and from all things from which ye could not be justified in the law of Moses, in him every one that believes is justified.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: See therefore that that which is spoken in the prophets do not come upon [you],

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, ye despisers, and wonder and perish; for I work a work in your days, a work which ye will in no wise believe if one declare it to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they went out they begged that these words might be spoken to them the ensuing sabbath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the congregation of the synagogue having broken up, many of the Jews and of the worshipping proselytes followed Paul and Barnabas, who speaking to them, persuaded them to continue in the grace ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the coming sabbath almost all the city was gathered together to hear the word ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the Jews, seeing the crowds, were filled with envy, and contradicted the things said by Paul, [contradicting and] speaking injuriously.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Paul and Barnabas spoke boldly and said, It was necessary that the word ofGod should be first spoken to you; but, since ye thrust it from you, and judge yourselves unworthy of eternal life, lo, we turn to the nations;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for thus has the Lord enjoined us: I have set thee for a light of the nations, that thou shouldest be for salvation to the end of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [those of] the nations, hearing it, rejoiced, and glorified the word of the Lord, and believed, as many as were ordained to eternal life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of the Lord was carried through the whole country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the Jews excited the women of the upper classes who were worshippers, and the first people of the city, and raised a persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and cast them out of their coasts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they, having shaken off the dust of their feet against them, came to Iconium.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the disciples were filled with joy and [the] Holy Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in Iconium that they entered together into the synagogue of the Jews, and so spake that a great multitude of both Jews and Greeks believed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the Jews who did not believe stirred up the minds of [those of] the nations and made [them] evil-affected against the brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They stayed therefore a good while, speaking boldly, [confiding] in the Lord, who gave witness to the word of his grace, giving signs and wonders to be done by their hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the multitude of the city was divided, and some were with the Jews and some with the apostles.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when an assault was making, both of [those of] the nations and [the] Jews with their rulers, to use [them] ill and stone them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: they, being aware of it, fled to the cities of Lycaonia, Lystra and Derbe, and the surrounding country,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and there they were announcing the glad tidings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a certain man in Lystra, impotent in his feet, sat, [being] lame from his mother's womb, who had never walked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This [man] heard Paul speaking, who, fixing his eyes on him, and seeing that he had faith to be healed,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: said with a loud voice, Rise up straight upon thy feet: and he sprang up and walked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the crowds, who saw what Paul had done, lifted up their voices in Lycaonian, saying, Thegods, having made themselves like men, are come down to us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they called Barnabas Jupiter, and Paul Mercury, because he took the lead in speaking.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priest of Jupiter who was before the city, having brought bulls and garlands to the gates, would have done sacrifice along with the crowds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the apostles Barnabas and Paul, having heard [it], rent their garments, and rushed out to the crowd, crying

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and saying, Men, why do ye these things? We also are men of like passions with you, preaching to you to turn from these vanities to the livingGod, who made the heaven, and the earth, and the sea, and all things in them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who in the past generations suffered all the nations to go in their own ways,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: though indeed he did not leave himself without witness, doing good, and giving to you from heaven rain and fruitful seasons, filling your hearts with food and gladness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And saying these things, they with difficulty kept the crowds from sacrificing to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But there came Jews from Antioch and Iconium, and having persuaded the crowds and stoned Paul, drew him out of the city, supposing him to have died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But while the disciples encircled him, he rose up and entered into the city. And on the morrow he went away with Barnabas to Derbe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having announced the glad tidings to that city, and having made many disciples, they returned to Lystra, and Iconium, and Antioch,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: establishing the souls of the disciples, exhorting them to abide in the faith, and that through many tribulations we must enter into the kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having chosen them elders in each assembly, having prayed with fastings, they committed them to the Lord, on whom they had believed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having passed through Pisidia they came to Pamphylia,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having spoken the word in Perga, they came down to Attalia;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thence they sailed away to Antioch, whence they had been committed to the grace ofGod for the work which they had fulfilled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having arrived, and having brought together the assembly, they related to them all thatGod had done with them, and that he had opened a door of faith to the nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they stayed no little time with the disciples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And certain persons, having come down from Judaea, taught the brethren, If ye shall not have been circumcised according to the custom of Moses, ye cannot be saved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A commotion therefore having taken place, and no small discussion on the part of Paul and Barnabas against them, they arranged that Paul and Barnabas, and certain others from amongst them, should go up to Jerusalem to the apostles and elders about this question.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They therefore, having been set on their way by the assembly, passed through Phoenicia and Samaria, relating the conversion of [those of] the nations. And they caused great joy to all the brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And being arrived at Jerusalem, they were received by the assembly, and the apostles, and the elders, and related all thatGod had wrought with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And some of those who were of the sect of the Pharisees, who believed, rose up from among [them], saying that they ought to circumcise them and enjoin them to keep the law of Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the apostles and the elders were gathered together to see about this matter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And much discussion having taken place, Peter, standing up, said to them, Brethren, ye know that from the earliest daysGod amongst you chose that the nations by my mouth should hear the word of the glad tidings and believe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the heart-knowingGod bore them witness, giving [them] the Holy Spirit as to us also,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and put no difference between us and them, having purified their hearts by faith.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now therefore why tempt yeGod, by putting a yoke upon the neck of the disciples, which neither our fathers nor we have been able to bear?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we believe that we shall be saved by the grace of the Lord Jesus, in the same manner as they also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the multitude kept silence and listened to Barnabas and Paul relating all the signs and wonders whichGod had wrought among the nations by them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after they had held their peace, James answered, saying, Brethren, listen to me:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Simon has related howGod first visited to take out of [the] nations a people for his name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And with this agree the words of the prophets; as it is written:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: After these things I will return, and will rebuild the tabernacle of David which is fallen, and will rebuild its ruins, and will set it up,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so that the residue of men may seek out the Lord, and all the nations on whom my name is invoked, saith [the] Lord, who does these things

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: known from eternity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore I judge, not to trouble those who from the nations turn toGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but to write to them to abstain from pollutions of idols, and from fornication, and from what is strangled, and from blood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Moses, from generations of old, has in every city those who preach him, being read in the synagogues every sabbath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then it seemed good to the apostles and to the elders, with the whole assembly, to send chosen men from among them with Paul and Barnabas to Antioch, Judas called Barsabas and Silas, leading men among the brethren,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: having by their hand written [thus]: The apostles, and the elders, and the brethren, to the brethren who are from among [the] nations at Antioch, and [in] Syria and Cilicia, greeting:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Inasmuch as we have heard that some who went out from amongst us have troubled you by words, upsetting your souls, [saying that ye must be circumcised and keep the law]; to whom we gave no commandment;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: it seemed good to us, having arrived at a common judgment, to send chosen men to you with our beloved Barnabas and Paul,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: men who have given up their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We have therefore sent Judas and Silas, who themselves also will tell you by word [of mouth] the same things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it has seemed good to the Holy Spirit and to us to lay upon you no greater burden than these necessary things:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to abstain from things sacrificed to idols, and from blood, and from what is strangled, and from fornication; keeping yourselves from which ye will do well. Farewell.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They therefore, being let go, came to Antioch, and having gathered the multitude delivered to [them] the epistle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having read it, they rejoiced at the consolation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judas and Silas, being themselves also prophets, exhorted the brethren with much discourse, and strengthened them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having passed some time [there], they were let go in peace from the brethren to those who sent them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Paul and Barnabas stayed in Antioch, teaching and announcing the glad tidings, with many others also, of the word of the Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But after certain days Paul said to Barnabas, Let us return now and visit the brethren in every city where we have announced the word of the Lord, [and see] how they are getting on.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Barnabas proposed to take with [them] John also, called Mark;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but Paul thought it not well to take with them him who had abandoned them, [going back] from Pamphylia, and had not gone with them to the work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There arose therefore very warm feeling, so that they separated from one another; and Barnabas taking Mark sailed away to Cyprus;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but Paul having chosen Silas went forth, committed by the brethren to the grace ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he passed through Syria and Cilicia, confirming the assemblies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he came to Derbe and Lystra: and behold, a certain disciple was there, by name Timotheus, son of a Jewish believing woman, but [the] father a Greek,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who had a [good] testimony of the brethren in Lystra and Iconium.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Him would Paul have go forth with him, and took [him and] circumcised him on account of the Jews who were in those places, for they all knew his father that he was a Greek.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they passed through the cities they instructed them to observe the decrees determined on by the apostles and elders who were in Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The assemblies therefore were confirmed in the faith, and increased in number every day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having passed through Phrygia and the Galatian country, having been forbidden by the Holy Spirit to speak the word in Asia,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: having come down to Mysia, they attempted to go to Bithynia, and the Spirit of Jesus did not allow them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having passed by Mysia they descended to Troas.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a vision appeared to Paul in the night: There was a certain Macedonian man, standing and beseeching him, and saying, Pass over into Macedonia and help us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he had seen the vision, immediately we sought to go forth to Macedonia, concluding that the Lord had called us to announce to them the glad tidings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having sailed therefore away from Troas, we went in a straight course to Samothracia, and on the morrow to Neapolis,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thence to Philippi, which is [the] first city of that part of Macedonia, a colony. And we were staying in that city certain days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the sabbath day we went outside the gate by the river, where it was the custom for prayer to be, and we sat down and spoke to the women who had assembled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a certain woman, by name Lydia, a seller of purple, of the city of Thyatira, who worshippedGod, heard; whose heart the Lord opened to attend to the things spoken by Paul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when she had been baptised and her house, she besought [us], saying, If ye have judged me to be faithful to the Lord, come into my house and abide [there]. And she constrained us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as we were going to prayer that a certain female slave, having a spirit of Python, met us, who brought much profit to her masters by prophesying.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She, having followed Paul and us, cried saying, These men are bondmen of the Most HighGod, who announce to you [the] way of salvation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this she did many days. And Paul, being distressed, turned, and said to the spirit, I enjoin thee in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her. And it came out the same hour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And her masters, seeing that the hope of their gains was gone, having seized Paul and Silas, dragged [them] into the market before the magistrates;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having brought them up to the praetors, said, These men utterly trouble our city, being Jews,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and announce customs which it is not lawful for us to receive nor practise, being Romans.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the crowd rose up too against them; and the praetors, having torn off their clothes, commanded to scourge [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having laid many stripes upon them they cast [them] into prison, charging the jailor to keep them safely;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who, having received such a charge, cast them into the inner prison, and secured their feet to the stocks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And at midnight Paul and Silas, in praying, were praisingGod with singing, and the prisoners listened to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And suddenly there was a great earthquake, so that the foundations of the prison shook, and all the doors were immediately opened, and the bonds of all loosed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the jailor being awakened out of his sleep, and seeing the doors of the prison opened, having drawn a sword was going to kill himself, thinking the prisoners had fled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Paul called out with a loud voice, saying, Do thyself no harm, for we are all here.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having asked for lights, he rushed in, and, trembling, fell down before Paul and Silas.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And leading them out said, Sirs, what must I do that I may be saved?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, Believe on the Lord Jesus and thou shalt be saved, thou and thy house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they spoke to him the word of the Lord, with all that were in his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he took them the same hour of the night and washed [them] from their stripes; and was baptised, he and all his straightway.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having brought them into his house he laid the table [for them], and rejoiced with all his house, having believed inGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when it was day, the praetors sent the lictors, saying, Let those men go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the jailor reported these words to Paul: The praetors have sent that ye may be let go. Now therefore go out and depart in peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Paul said to them, Having beaten us publicly uncondemned, us who are Romans, they have cast us into prison, and now they thrust us out secretly? no, indeed, but let them come themselves and bring us out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the lictors reported these words to the praetors. And they were afraid when they heard they were Romans.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they came and besought them, and having brought them out, asked them to go out of the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having gone out of the prison, they came to Lydia; and having seen the brethren, they exhorted them and went away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having journeyed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where was the synagogue of the Jews.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And according to Paul's custom he went in among them, and on three sabbaths reasoned with them from the scriptures,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: opening and laying down that the Christ must have suffered and risen up from among the dead, and that this is the Christ, Jesus whom I announce to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And some of them believed, and joined themselves to Paul and Silas, and of the Greeks who worshipped, a great multitude, and of the chief women not a few.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the Jews having been stirred up to jealousy, and taken to [themselves] certain wicked men of the lowest rabble, and having got a crowd together, set the city in confusion; and having beset the house of Jason sought to bring them out to the people;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and not having found them, dragged Jason and certain brethren before the politarchs, crying out, These [men] that have set the world in tumult, are come here also,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: whom Jason has received; and these all do contrary to the decrees of Caesar, saying, that there is another king, Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they troubled the crowd and the politarchs when they heard these things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having taken security of Jason and the rest, they let them go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the brethren immediately sent away, in the night, Paul and Silas to Berea; who, being arrived, went away into the synagogue of the Jews.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these were more noble than those in Thessalonica, receiving the word with all readiness of mind, daily searching the scriptures if these things were so.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore many from among them believed, and of Grecian women of the upper classes and men not a few.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when the Jews from Thessalonica knew that the word ofGod was announced in Berea also by Paul, they came there also, stirring up the crowds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And then immediately the brethren sent away Paul to go as to the sea; but Silas and Timotheus abode there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they that conducted Paul brought him as far as Athens; and, having received a commandment to Silas and Timotheus, that they should come to him as quickly as possible, they departed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But in Athens, while Paul was waiting for them, his spirit was painfully excited in him seeing the city given up to idolatry.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He reasoned therefore in the synagogue with the Jews, and those who worshipped, and in the market-place every day with those he met with.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But some also of the Epicurean and Stoic philosophers attacked him. And some said, What would this chatterer say? and some, He seems to be an announcer of foreign demons, because he announced the glad tidings of Jesus and the resurrection [to them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having taken hold on him they brought [him] to Areopagus, saying, Might we know what this new doctrine which is spoken by thee [is]?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou bringest certain strange things to our ears. We wish therefore to know what these things may mean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now all [the] Athenians and the strangers sojourning there spent their time in nothing else than to tell and to hear the news.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Paul standing in the midst of Areopagus said, Athenians, in every way I see you given up to demon worship;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for, passing through and beholding your shrines, I found also an altar on which was inscribed, To the unknownGod. Whom therefore ye reverence, not knowing [him], him I announce to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: TheGod who has made the world and all things which are in it, he, being Lord of heaven and earth, does not dwell in temples made with hands,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: nor is served by men's hands as needing something, himself giving to all life and breath and all things;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and has made of one blood every nation of men to dwell upon the whole face of the earth, having determined ordained times and the boundaries of their dwelling,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that they may seekGod; if indeed they might feel after him and find him, although he is not far from each one of us:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for in him we live and move and exist; as also some of the poets amongst you have said, For we are also his offspring.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Being therefore [the] offspring ofGod, we ought not to think that which is divine to be like gold or silver or stone, [the] graven form of man's art and imagination.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God therefore, having overlooked the times of ignorance, now enjoins men that they shall all everywhere repent,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because he has set a day in which he is going to judge the habitable earth in righteousness by [the] man whom he has appointed, giving the proof [of it] to all [in] having raised him from among [the] dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they heard [of the] resurrection of the dead, some mocked, and some said, We will hear thee again also concerning this.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus Paul went out of their midst.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But some men joining themselves to him believed; among whom also was Dionysius the Areopagite, and a woman by name Damaris, and others with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after these things, having left Athens, he came to Corinth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and finding a certain Jew by name Aquila, of Pontus by race, just come from Italy, and Priscilla his wife, (because Claudius had ordered all the Jews to leave Rome,) came to them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and because they were of the same trade abode with them, and wrought. For they were tent-makers by trade.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he reasoned in the synagogue every sabbath, and persuaded Jews and Greeks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when both Silas and Timotheus came down from Macedonia, Paul was pressed in respect of the word, testifying to the Jews that Jesus was the Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as they opposed and spoke injuriously, he shook his clothes, and said to them, Your blood be upon your own head: I [am] pure; from henceforth I will go to the nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And departing thence he came to the house of a certain [man], by name Justus, who worshippedGod, whose house adjoined the synagogue.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Crispus the ruler of the synagogue believed in the Lord with all his house; and many of the Corinthians hearing, believed, and were baptised.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Lord said by vision in [the] night to Paul, Fear not, but speak and be not silent;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because I am with thee, and no one shall set upon thee to injure thee; because I have much people in this city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he remained [there] a year and six months, teaching among them the word ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when Gallio was proconsul of Achaia, the Jews with one consent rose against Paul and led him to the judgment-seat,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, This [man] persuades men to worshipGod contrary to the law.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as Paul was going to open his mouth, Gallio said to the Jews, If indeed it was some wrong or wicked criminality, O Jews, of reason I should have borne with you;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but if it be questions about words, and names, and the law that ye have, see to it yourselves; [for] I do not intend to be judge of these things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he drove them from the judgment-seat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having all laid hold on Sosthenes the ruler of the synagogue, they beat him before the judgment-seat. And Gallio troubled himself about none of these things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Paul, having yet stayed [there] many days, took leave of the brethren and sailed thence to Syria, and with him Priscilla and Aquila, having shorn his head in Cenchrea, for he had a vow;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he arrived at Ephesus, and left them there. But entering himself into the synagogue he reasoned with the Jews.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they asked him that he would remain for a longer time [with them] he did not accede,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but bade them farewell, saying, [I must by all means keep the coming feast at Jerusalem]; I will return to you again, ifGod will: and he sailed away from Ephesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And landing at Caesarea, and having gone up and saluted the assembly, he went down to Antioch.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having stayed [there] some time, he went forth, passing in order through the country of Galatia and Phrygia, establishing all the disciples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But a certain Jew, Apollos by name, an Alexandrian by race, an eloquent man, who was mighty in the scriptures, arrived at Ephesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He was instructed in the way of the Lord, and being fervent in his spirit, he spoke and taught exactly the things concerning Jesus, knowing only the baptism of John.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he began to speak boldly in the synagogue. And Aquila and Priscilla, having heard him, took him to [them] and unfolded to him the way ofGod more exactly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he purposed to go into Achaia, the brethren wrote to the disciples engaging them to receive him, who, being come, contributed much to those who believed through grace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he with great force convinced the Jews publicly, shewing by the scriptures that Jesus was the Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, while Apollos was at Corinth, Paul, having passed through the upper districts, came to Ephesus, and finding certain disciples,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he said to them, Did ye receive [the] Holy Spirit when ye had believed? And they [said] to him, We did not even hear if [the] Holy Spirit was [come].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, To what then were ye baptised? And they said, To the baptism of John.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Paul said, John indeed baptised [with] the baptism of repentance, saying to the people that they should believe on him that was coming after him, that is, on Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they heard that, they were baptised to the name of the Lord Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Paul having laid [his] hands on them, the Holy Spirit came upon them, and they spoke with tongues and prophesied.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the men were about twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And entering into the synagogue, he spoke boldly during three months, reasoning and persuading [the things] concerning the kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when some were hardened and disbelieved, speaking evil of the way before the multitude, he left them and separated the disciples, reasoning daily in the school of Tyrannus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this took place for two years, so that all that inhabited Asia heard the word of the Lord, both Jews and Greeks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod wrought no ordinary miracles by the hands of Paul,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so that even napkins or aprons were brought from his body [and put] upon the sick, and the diseases left them, and the wicked spirits went out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And certain of the Jewish exorcists also, who went about, took in hand to call upon those who had wicked spirits the name of the Lord Jesus, saying, I adjure you by Jesus, whom Paul preaches.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there were certain [men], seven sons of Sceva, Jewish high priest, who were doing this.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the wicked spirit answering said to them, Jesus I know, and Paul I am acquainted with; but ye, who are ye?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man in whom the wicked spirit was leaped upon them, and having mastered both, prevailed against them, so that they fled out of that house naked and wounded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this became known to all, both Jews and Greeks, who inhabited Ephesus, and fear fell upon all of them, and the name of the Lord Jesus was magnified.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And many of those that believed came confessing and declaring their deeds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And many of those that practised curious arts brought their books [of charms] and burnt them before all. And they reckoned up the prices of them, and found it fifty thousand pieces of silver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus with might the word of the Lord increased and prevailed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when these things were fulfilled, Paul purposed in his spirit to go to Jerusalem, passing through Macedonia and Achaia, saying, After I have been there I must see Rome also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having sent into Macedonia two of those ministering to him, Timotheus and Erastus, he remained himself awhile in Asia.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there took place at that time no small disturbance about the way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For a certain [man] by name Demetrius, a silver-beater, making silver temples of Artemis, brought no small gain to the artisans;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: whom having brought together, and those who wrought in such things, he said, Men, ye know that our well-living arises from this work,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye see and hear that this Paul has persuaded and turned away a great crowd, not only of Ephesus, but almost of all Asia, saying that they are nogods which are made with hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now not only there is danger for us that our business come into discredit, but also that the temple of the greatgoddess Artemis be counted for nothing, and that her greatness should be destroyed whom the whole of Asia and the world reveres.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having heard [this], and being filled with rage, they cried out, saying, Great [is] Artemis of the Ephesians.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the [whole] city was filled with confusion, and they rushed with one accord to the theatre, having seized and carried off with [them] Gaius and Aristarchus, Macedonians, fellow-travellers of Paul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Paul intending to go in to the people, the disciples suffered him not;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and some of the Asiarchs also, who were his friends, sent to him and urged him not to throw himself into the theatre.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Different persons therefore cried out some different thing; for the assembly was tumultuous, and the most did not know for what cause they had come together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But from among the crowd they put forward Alexander, the Jews pushing him forward. And Alexander, beckoning with his hand, would have made a defence to the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But, recognising that he was a Jew, there was one cry from all, shouting for about two hours, Great [is] Artemis of the Ephesians.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the townclerk, having quieted the crowd, said, Ephesians, what man is there then who does not know that the city of the Ephesians is temple-keeper of Artemis the great, and of the [image] which fell down from heaven?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These things therefore being undeniable, it is necessary that ye should be calm and do nothing headlong.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ye have brought these men, [who are] neither temple-plunderers, nor speak injuriously of yourgoddess.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If therefore Demetrius and the artisans who [are] with him have a matter against any one, the courts are being held, and there are proconsuls: let them accuse one another.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if ye inquire anything concerning other matters, it will be settled in the regular assembly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For also we are in danger to be put in accusation for sedition for this [affair] of to-day, no cause existing in reference to which we shall be able to give a reason for this concourse.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having said these things, he dismissed the assembly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But after the tumult had ceased, Paul having called the disciples to [him] and embraced [them], went away to go to Macedonia.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having passed through those parts, and having exhorted them with much discourse, he came to Greece.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having spent three months [there], a treacherous plot against him having been set on foot by the Jews, as he was going to sail to Syria, [the] resolution was adopted of returning through Macedonia.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there accompanied him as far as Asia, Sopater [son] of Pyrrhus, a Berean; and of Thessalonians, Aristarchus and Secundus, and Gaius and Timotheus of Derbe, and of Asia, Tychicus and Trophimus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These going before waited for us in Troas;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but we sailed away from Philippi after the days of unleavened bread, and we came to them to Troas in five days, where we spent seven days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the first day of the week, we being assembled to break bread, Paul discoursed to them, about to depart on the morrow. And he prolonged the discourse till midnight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there were many lights in the upper room where we were assembled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a certain youth, by name Eutychus, sitting at the window-opening, overpowered by deep sleep, while Paul discoursed very much at length, having been overpowered by the sleep, fell from the third story down to the bottom, and was taken up dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Paul descending fell upon him, and enfolding [him] [in his arms], said, Be not troubled, for his life is in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having gone up, and having broken the bread, and eaten, and having long spoken until daybreak, so he went away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they brought [away] the boy alive, and were no little comforted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we, having gone before on board ship, sailed off to Assos, going to take in Paul there; for so he had directed, he himself being about to go on foot.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he met with us at Assos, having taken him on board, we came to Mitylene;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having sailed thence, on the morrow arrived opposite Chios, and the next day put in at Samos; and having stayed at Trogyllium, the next day we came to Miletus:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Paul thought it desirable to sail by Ephesus, so that he might not be made to spend time in Asia; for he hastened, if it was possible for him, to be the day of Pentecost at Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But from Miletus having sent to Ephesus, he called over [to him] the elders of the assembly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they were come to him, he said to them, Ye know how I was with you all the time from the first day that I arrived in Asia,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: serving the Lord with all lowliness, and tears, and temptations, which happened to me through the plots of the Jews;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: how I held back nothing of what is profitable, so as not to announce [it] to you, and to teach you publicly and in every house,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: testifying to both Jews and Greeks repentance towardsGod, and faith towards our Lord Jesus Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, behold, bound in my spirit I go to Jerusalem, not knowing what things shall happen to me in it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: only that the Holy Spirit testifies to me in every city, saying that bonds and tribulations await me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I make no account of [my] life [as] dear to myself, so that I finish my course, and the ministry which I have received of the Lord Jesus, to testify the glad tidings of the grace ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, behold, I know that ye all, among whom I have gone about preaching the kingdom [ofGod], shall see my face no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore I witness to you this day, that I am clean from the blood of all,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for I have not shrunk from announcing to you all the counsel ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take heed therefore to yourselves, and to all the flock, wherein the Holy Spirit has set you as overseers, to shepherd the assembly ofGod, which he has purchased with the blood of his own.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [For] I know [this,] that there will come in amongst you after my departure grievous wolves, not sparing the flock;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and from among your own selves shall rise up men speaking perverted things to draw away the disciples after them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore watch, remembering that for three years, night and day, I ceased not admonishing each one [of you] with tears.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now I commit you toGod, and to the word of his grace, which is able to build [you] up and give [to you] an inheritance among all the sanctified.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have coveted [the] silver or gold or clothing of no one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yourselves know that these hands have ministered to my wants, and to those who were with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have shewed you all things, that thus labouring [we] ought to come in aid of the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, that he himself said, It is more blessed to give than to receive.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having said these things, he knelt down and prayed with them all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they all wept sore; and falling upon the neck of Paul they ardently kissed him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: specially pained by the word which he had said, that they would no more see his face. And they went down with him to the ship.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when, having got away from them, we at last sailed away, we came by a direct course to Cos, and on the morrow to Rhodes, and thence to Patara.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having found a ship passing over into Phoenicia, we went on board and sailed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having sighted Cyprus, and left it on the left hand, we sailed to Syria, and made the land at Tyre, for there the ship was to discharge her cargo.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having found out the disciples, we remained there seven days; who said to Paul by the Spirit not to go up to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when we had completed the days, we set out and took our journey, all of them accompanying us, with wives and children, till [we were] out of the city. And kneeling down upon the shore we prayed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having embraced one another, we went on board ship, and they returned home.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we, having completed the voyage, arrived from Tyre at Ptolemais, and having saluted the brethren, we remained one day with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And leaving on the morrow, we came to Caesarea; and entering into the house of Philip the evangelist, who was of the seven, we abode with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now this man had four virgin daughters who prophesied.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as we stayed there many days, a certain man, by name Agabus, a prophet, came down from Judaea,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and coming to us and taking the girdle of Paul, and having bound his own hands and feet, said, Thus saith the Holy Spirit, The man whose this girdle is shall the Jews thus bind in Jerusalem, and deliver him up into the hands of [the] Gentiles.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when we heard these things, both we and those of the place besought [him] not to go up to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Paul answered, What do ye, weeping and breaking my heart? for I am ready not only to be bound, but also to die at Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he would not be persuaded, we were silent, saying, The will of the Lord be done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after these days, having got our effects ready, we went up to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [some] of the disciples from Caesarea went with us, bringing [with them] a certain Mnason, a Cyprian, an old disciple, with whom we were to lodge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when we arrived at Jerusalem the brethren gladly received us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the morrow Paul went in with us to James, and all the elders came there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having saluted them, he related one by one the things whichGod had wrought among the nations by his ministry.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they having heard [it] glorifiedGod, and said to him, Thou seest, brother, how many myriads there are of the Jews who have believed, and all are zealous of the law.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they have been informed concerning thee, that thou teachest all the Jews among the nations apostasy from Moses, saying that they should not circumcise their children, nor walk in the customs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What is it then? a multitude must necessarily come together, for they will hear that thou art come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This do therefore that we say to thee: We have four men who have a vow on them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: take these and be purified with them, and pay their expenses, that they may have their heads shaved; and all will know that [of those things] of which they have been informed about thee nothing is [true]; but that thou thyself also walkest orderly, keeping the law.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But concerning [those of] the nations who have believed, we have written, deciding that they should [observe no such thing, only to] keep themselves both from things offered to idols, and from blood, and from things strangled, and from fornication.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Paul, taking the men, on the next day, having been purified, entered with them into the temple, signifying the time the days of the purification would be fulfilled, until the offering was offered for every one of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the seven days were nearly completed, the Jews from Asia, having seen him in the temple, set all the crowd in a tumult, and laid hands upon him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: crying, Israelites, help! this is the man who teaches all everywhere against the people, and the law, and this place, and has brought Greeks too into the temple, and profaned this holy place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they had before seen Trophimus the Ephesian with him in the city, whom they supposed that Paul had brought into the temple.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the whole city was moved, and there was a concourse of the people; and having laid hold on Paul they drew him out of the temple, and immediately the doors were shut.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they were seeking to kill him, a representation came to the chiliarch of the band that the whole of Jerusalem was in a tumult;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who, taking with him immediately soldiers and centurions, ran down upon them. But they, seeing the chiliarch and the soldiers, ceased beating Paul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the chiliarch came up and laid hold upon him, and commanded [him] to be bound with two chains, and inquired who he might be, and what he had done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And different persons cried some different thing in the crowd. But he, not being able to know the certainty on account of the uproar, commanded him to be brought into the fortress.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when he got upon the stairs it was so that he was borne by the soldiers on account of the violence of the crowd.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the multitude of the people followed, crying, Away with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as he was about to be led into the fortress, Paul says to the chiliarch, Is it allowed me to say something to thee? And he said, Dost thou know Greek?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou art not then that Egyptian who before these days raised a sedition and led out into the wilderness the four thousand men of the assassins?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Paul said, I am a Jew of Tarsus, citizen of no insignificant city of Cilicia, and I beseech of thee, allow me to speak to the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he had allowed him, Paul, standing on the stairs, beckoned with his hand to the people; and a great silence having been made, he addressed them in the Hebrew tongue, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Brethren and fathers, hear my defence which I now make to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And hearing that he addressed them in the Hebrew tongue, they kept the more quiet; and he says,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but brought up in this city, at the feet of Gamaliel, educated according to [the] exactness of the law of [our] fathers, being zealous forGod, as ye are all this day;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who have persecuted this way unto death, binding and delivering up to prisons both men and women;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: as also the high priest bears me witness, and all the elderhood: from whom also, having received letters to the brethren, I went to Damascus to bring those also who were there, bound, to Jerusalem, to be punished.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, as I was journeying and drawing near to Damascus, that, about mid-day, there suddenly shone out of heaven a great light round about me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I fell to the ground, and heard a voice saying to me, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I answered, Who art thou, Lord? And he said to me, I am Jesus the Nazaraean, whom thou persecutest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they that were with me beheld the light, [and were filled with fear], but heard not the voice of him that was speaking to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said, What shall I do, Lord? And the Lord said to me, Rise up, and go to Damascus, and there it shall be told thee of all things which it is appointed thee to do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as I could not see, through the glory of that light, being led by the hand of those who were with me, I came to Damascus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a certain Ananias, a pious man according to the law, borne witness to by all the Jews who dwelt [there],

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: coming to me and standing by me, said to me, Brother Saul, receive thy sight. And I, in the same hour, received my sight and saw him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, TheGod of our fathers has chosen thee beforehand to know his will, and to see the just one, and to hear a voice out of his mouth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for thou shalt be a witness for him to all men of what thou hast seen and heard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now why lingerest thou? Arise and get baptised, and have thy sins washed away, calling on his name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when I had returned to Jerusalem, and as I was praying in the temple, that I became in ecstasy,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and saw him saying to me, Make haste and go quickly out of Jerusalem, for they will not receive thy testimony concerning me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said, Lord, they themselves know that I was imprisoning and beating in every synagogue those that believe on thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and when the blood of thy witness Stephen was shed, I also myself was standing by and consenting, and kept the clothes of them who killed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to me, Go, for I will send thee to the nations afar off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they heard him until this word, and lifted up their voice, saying, Away with such a one as that from the earth, for it was not fit he should live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they were crying, and throwing away their clothes, and casting dust into the air,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the chiliarch commanded him to be brought into the fortress, saying that he should be examined by scourging, that he might ascertain for what cause they cried thus against him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as they stretched him forward with the thongs, Paul said to the centurion who stood [by], Is it lawful for you to scourge a man [who is] a Roman and uncondemned?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the centurion, having heard it, went and reported it to the chiliarch, saying, What art thou going to do? for this man is a Roman.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the chiliarch coming up said to him, Tell me, Art thou a Roman? And he said, Yes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the chiliarch answered, I, for a great sum, bought this citizenship. And Paul said, But I was also [free] born.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Immediately therefore those who were going to examine him left him, and the chiliarch also was afraid when he ascertained that he was a Roman, and because he had bound him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the morrow, desirous to know the certainty [of the matter] why he was accused of the Jews, he loosed him, and commanded the chief priests and all the council to meet, and having brought Paul down set him before them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Paul, fixing his eyes on the council, said, Brethren, I have walked in all good conscience withGod unto this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the high priest Ananias ordered those standing by him to smite his mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Paul said to him,God will smite thee, whited wall. And thou, dost thou sit judging me according to the law, and breaking the law commandest me to be smitten?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those that stood by said, Dost thou rail against the high priest ofGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Paul said, I was not conscious, brethren, that he was high priest; for it is written, Thou shalt not speak evilly of the ruler of thy people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Paul, knowing that the one part [of them] were of the Sadducees and the other of the Pharisees, cried out in the council, Brethren, I am a Pharisee, son of Pharisees: I am judged concerning the hope and resurrection of [the] dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he had spoken this, there was a tumult of the Pharisees and the Sadducees, and the multitude was divided.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Sadducees say there is no resurrection, nor angel, nor spirit; but Pharisees confess both of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was a great clamour, and the scribes of the Pharisees' part rising up contended, saying, We find nothing evil in this man; and if a spirit has spoken to him, or an angel...

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a great tumult having arisen, the chiliarch, fearing lest Paul should have been torn in pieces by them, commanded the troop to come down and take him by force from the midst of them, and to bring [him] into the fortress.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the following night the Lord stood by him, and said, Be of good courage; for as thou hast testified the things concerning me at Jerusalem, so thou must bear witness at Rome also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when it was day, the Jews, having banded together, put themselves under a curse, saying that they would neither eat nor drink till they should kill Paul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were more than forty who had joined together in this oath;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they went to the chief priests and elders, and said, We have cursed ourselves with a curse to taste nothing until we kill Paul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now therefore do ye with the council make a representation to the chiliarch so that he may bring him down to you, as about to determine more precisely what concerns him, and we, before he draws near, are ready to kill him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Paul's sister's son, having heard of the lying in wait, came and entered into the fortress and reported [it] to Paul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Paul, having called one of the centurions, said, Take this youth to the chiliarch, for he has something to report to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He therefore, having taken him with [him], led him to the chiliarch, and says, The prisoner Paul called me to [him] and asked me to lead this youth to thee, who has something to say to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the chiliarch having taken him by the hand, and having gone apart in private, inquired, What is it that thou hast to report to me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, The Jews have agreed together to make a request to thee, that thou mayest bring Paul down to-morrow into the council, as about to inquire something more precise concerning him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do not thou then be persuaded by them, for there lie in wait for him of them more than forty men, who have put themselves under a curse neither to eat nor drink till they kill him; and now they are ready waiting the promise from thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The chiliarch then dismissed the youth, commanding [him], Utter to no one that thou hast represented these things to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having called to [him] certain two of the centurions, he said, Prepare two hundred soldiers that they may go as far as Caesarea, and seventy horsemen, and two hundred light-armed footmen, for the third hour of the night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [he ordered them] to provide beasts, that they might set Paul on them and carry [him] safe through to Felix the governor,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: having written a letter, couched in this form:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Claudius Lysias to the most excellent governor Felix, greeting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This man, having been taken by the Jews, and being about to be killed by them, I came up with the military and took out [of their hands], having learned that he was a Roman.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And desiring to know the charge on which they accused him, I brought him down to their council;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: whom I found to be accused of questions of their law, but to have no charge laid against him [making him] worthy of death or of bonds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But having received information of a plot about to be put in execution against the man [by the Jews], I have immediately sent him to thee, commanding also his accusers to say before thee the things that are against him. [Farewell.]

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The soldiers therefore, according to what was ordered them, took Paul and brought him by night to Antipatris,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and on the morrow, having left the horsemen to go with him, returned to the fortress.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these, having entered into Caesarea, and given up the letter to the governor, presented Paul also to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having read [it], and asked of what eparchy he was, and learned that [he was] of Cilicia,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he said, I will hear thee fully when thine accusers also are arrived. And he commanded him to be kept in Herod's praetorium.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after five days came down the high priest Ananias, with the elders, and a certain orator called Tertullus, and laid their informations against Paul before the governor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he having been called, Tertullus began to accuse, saying, Seeing we enjoy great peace through thee, and that excellent measures are executed for this nation by thy forethought,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: we receive [it] always and everywhere, most excellent Felix, with all thankfulness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But that I may not too much intrude on thy time, I beseech thee to hear us briefly in thy kindness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For finding this man a pest, and moving sedition among all the Jews throughout the world, and a leader of the sect of the Nazaraeans;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who also attempted to profane the temple; whom we also had seized, [and would have judged according to our law;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but Lysias, the chiliarch, coming up, took [him] away with great force out of our hands,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: having commanded his accusers to come to thee;] of whom thou canst thyself, in examining [him], know the certainty of all these things of which we accuse him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Jews also joined in pressing the matter against [Paul], saying that these things were so.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Paul, the governor having beckoned to him to speak, answered, Knowing that for many years thou hast been judge to this nation, I answer readily as to the things which concern myself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As thou mayest know that there are not more than twelve days since I went up to worship at Jerusalem,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and neither in the temple did they find me discoursing to any one, or making any tumultuous gathering together of the crowd, nor in the synagogues, nor in the city;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: neither can they make good the things of which they now accuse me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But this I avow to thee, that in the way which they call sect, so I serve my fathers'God, believing all things which are written throughout the law, and in the prophets;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: having hope towardsGod, which they themselves also receive, that there is to be a resurrection both of just and unjust.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this cause I also exercise [myself] to have in everything a conscience without offence towardsGod and men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after a lapse of many years I arrived, bringing alms to my nation, and offerings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whereupon they found me purified in the temple, with neither crowd nor tumult. But it was certain Jews from Asia,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who ought to appear before thee and accuse, if they have anything against me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: or let these themselves say what wrong they found in me when I stood before the council,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [other] than concerning this one voice which I cried standing amongst them: I am judged this day by you touching [the] resurrection of [the] dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Felix, knowing accurately the things concerning the way, adjourned them, saying, When Lysias the chiliarch is come down I will determine your affair;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ordering the centurion to keep him, and that he should have freedom, and to hinder none of his friends to minister to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after certain days, Felix having arrived with Drusilla his wife, who was a Jewess, he sent for Paul and heard him concerning the faith in Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he reasoned concerning righteousness, and temperance, and the judgment about to come, Felix, being filled with fear, answered, Go for the present, and when I get an opportunity I will send for thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: hoping at the same time that money would be given him by Paul: wherefore also he sent for him the oftener and communed with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when two years were completed, Felix was relieved by Porcius Festus as his successor; and Felix, desirous to oblige the Jews, to acquire their favour, left Paul bound.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Festus therefore, being come into the eparchy, after three days went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the chief priests and the chief of the Jews laid informations before him against Paul, and besought him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: asking as a grace against him that he would send for him to Jerusalem, laying people in wait to kill him on the way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Festus therefore answered that Paul should be kept at Caesarea, and that he himself was about to set out shortly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let therefore the persons of authority among you, says he, going down too, if there be anything in this man, accuse him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having remained among them not more than eight or ten days, he went down to Caesarea; and on the next day, having sat down on the judgment-seat, commanded Paul to be brought.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he was come, the Jews who were come down from Jerusalem stood round, bringing many and grievous charges which they were not able to prove:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Paul answering for himself, Neither against the law of the Jews, nor against the temple, nor against Caesar, have I offended [in] anything.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Festus, desirous of obliging the Jews, to acquire their favour, answering Paul, said, Art thou willing to go up to Jerusalem, there to be judged before me concerning these things?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Paul said, I am standing before the judgment-seat of Caesar, where I ought to be judged. To the Jews have I done no wrong, as thou also very well knowest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If then I have done any wrong and committed anything worthy of death, I do not deprecate dying; but if there is nothing of those things of which they accuse me, no man can give me up to them. I appeal to Caesar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Festus, having conferred with the council, answered, Thou hast appealed to Caesar. To Caesar shalt thou go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when certain days had elapsed, Agrippa the king and Bernice arrived at Caesarea to salute Festus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they had spent many days there, Festus laid before the king the matters relating to Paul, saying, There is a certain man left prisoner by Felix,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: concerning whom, when I was at Jerusalem, the chief priests and the elders of the Jews laid informations, requiring judgment against him:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to whom I answered, It is not [the] custom of the Romans to give up any man before that the accused have the accusers face to face, and he have got opportunity of defence touching the charge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When therefore they had come together here, without putting it off, I sat the next day on the judgment-seat and commanded the man to be brought:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: concerning whom the accusers, standing up, brought no such accusation of guilt as I supposed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but had against him certain questions of their own system of worship, and concerning a certain Jesus who is dead, whom Paul affirmed to be living.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as I myself was at a loss as to an inquiry into these things, I said, Was he willing to go to Jerusalem and there to be judged concerning these things?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Paul having appealed to be kept for the cognisance of Augustus, I commanded him to be kept till I shall send him to Caesar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Agrippa [said] to Festus, I myself also would desire to hear the man. To-morrow, said he, thou shalt hear him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the morrow therefore, Agrippa being come, and Bernice, with great pomp, and having entered into the hall of audience, with the chiliarchs and the men of distinction of the city, and Festus having given command, Paul was brought.

translations.name: Darby Bible
