translations.text: And Festus said, King Agrippa, and all men who are here present with us, ye see this person, concerning whom all the multitude of the Jews applied to me both in Jerusalem and here, crying out against [him] that he ought not to live any longer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I, having found that he had done nothing worthy of death, and this [man] himself having appealed to Augustus, I have decided to send him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: concerning whom I have nothing certain to write to my lord. Wherefore I have brought him before you, and specially before thee, king Agrippa, so that an examination having been gone into I may have something to write:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for it seems to me senseless, sending a prisoner, not also to signify the charges against him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Agrippa said to Paul, It is permitted thee to speak for thyself. Then Paul stretching out his hand answered in his defence:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I count myself happy, king Agrippa, in having to answer to-day before thee concerning all of which I am accused by the Jews,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: especially because thou art acquainted with all the customs and questions which are among the Jews; wherefore I beseech thee to hear me patiently.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My manner of life then from my youth, which from its commencement was passed among my nation in Jerusalem, know all the Jews,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who knew me before from the outset [of my life], if they would bear witness, that according to the strictest sect of our religion I lived a Pharisee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now I stand to be judged because of the hope of the promise made byGod to our fathers,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to which our whole twelve tribes serving incessantly day and night hope to arrive; about which hope, O king, I am accused of [the] Jews.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why should it be judged a thing incredible in your sight ifGod raises the dead?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I indeed myself thought that I ought to do much against the name of Jesus the Nazaraean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Which also I did in Jerusalem, and myself shut up in prisons many of the saints, having received the authority from the chief priests; and when they were put to death I gave my vote.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And often punishing them in all the synagogues, I compelled them to blaspheme. And, being exceedingly furious against them, I persecuted them even to cities out [of our own land].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when, [engaged] in this, I was journeying to Damascus, with authority and power from the chief priests,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: at mid-day, on the way, I saw, O king, a light above the brightness of the sun, shining from heaven round about me and those who were journeying with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, when we were all fallen to the ground, I heard a voice saying to me in the Hebrew tongue, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me? [it is] hard for thee to kick against goads.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said, Who art thou, Lord? And the Lord said, I am Jesus whom thou persecutest:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but rise up and stand on thy feet; for, for this purpose have I appeared to thee, to appoint thee to be a servant and a witness both of what thou hast seen, and of what I shall appear to thee in,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: taking thee out from among the people, and the nations, to whom I send thee,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to open their eyes, that they may turn from darkness to light, and from the power of Satan toGod, that they may receive remission of sins and inheritance among them that are sanctified by faith in me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whereupon, king Agrippa, I was not disobedient to the heavenly vision;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but have, first to those both in Damascus and Jerusalem, and to all the region of Judaea, and to the nations, announced that they should repent and turn toGod, doing works worthy of repentance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On account of these things the Jews, having seized me in the temple, attempted to lay hands on and destroy me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having therefore met with [the] help which is fromGod, I have stood firm unto this day, witnessing both to small and great, saying nothing else than those things which both the prophets and Moses have said should happen,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [namely,] whether Christ should suffer; whether he first, through resurrection of [the] dead, should announce light both to the people and to the nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he answered for his defence with these things, Festus says with a loud voice, Thou art mad, Paul; much learning turns thee to madness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Paul said, I am not mad, most excellent Festus, but utter words of truth and soberness;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for the king is informed about these things, to whom also I speak with all freedom. For I am persuaded that of these things nothing is hidden from him; for this was not done in a corner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: King Agrippa, believest thou the prophets? I know that thou believest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Agrippa [said] to Paul, In a little thou persuadest me to become a Christian.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Paul [said], I would toGod, both in little and in much, that not only thou, but all who have heard me this day, should become such as I also am, except these bonds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king stood up, and the governor and Bernice, and those who sat with them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having gone apart, they spoke to one another saying, This man does nothing worthy of death or of bonds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Agrippa said to Festus, This man might have been let go if he had not appealed to Caesar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when it had been determined that we should sail to Italy, they delivered up Paul and certain other prisoners to a centurion, by name Julius, of Augustus' company.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And going on board a ship of Adramyttium about to navigate by the places along Asia, we set sail, Aristarchus, a Macedonian of Thessalonica, being with us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the next day we arrived at Sidon. And Julius treated Paul kindly and suffered him to go to his friends and refresh himself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And setting sail thence we sailed under the lee of Cyprus, because the winds were contrary.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having sailed over the waters of Cilicia and Pamphylia we came to Myra in Lycia:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and there the centurion having found a ship of Alexandria sailing to Italy, he made us go on board her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And sailing slowly for many days, and having with difficulty got abreast of Cnidus, the wind not suffering us, we sailed under the lee of Crete abreast of Salmone;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and coasting it with difficulty we came to a certain place called Fair Havens, near to which was [the] city of Lasaea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And much time having now been spent, and navigation being already dangerous, because the fast also was already past, Paul counselled them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, Men, I perceive that the navigation will be with disaster and much loss, not only of the cargo and the ship, but also of our lives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the centurion believed rather the helmsman and the shipowner than what was said by Paul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the harbour being ill adapted to winter in, the most counselled to set sail thence, if perhaps they might reach Phoenice to winter in, a port of Crete looking north-east and south-east.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [the] south wind blowing gently, supposing that they had gained their object, having weighed anchor they sailed close in shore along Crete.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But not long after there came down it a hurricane called Euroclydon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the ship being caught and driven, and not able to bring her head to the wind, letting her go we were driven [before it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But running under the lee of a certain island called Clauda, we were with difficulty able to make ourselves masters of the boat;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which having hoisted up, they used helps, frapping the ship; and fearing lest they should run into Syrtis and run aground, and having lowered the gear they were so driven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the storm being extremely violent on us, on the next day they threw cargo overboard,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and on the third day with their own hands they cast away the ship furniture.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And neither sun nor stars appearing for many days, and no small storm lying on us, in the end all hope of our being saved was taken away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they had been a long while without taking food, Paul then standing up in the midst of them said, Ye ought, O men, to have hearkened to me, and not have made sail from Crete and have gained this disaster and loss.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now I exhort you to be of good courage, for there shall be no loss at all of life of [any] of you, only of the ship.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For an angel of theGod, whose I am and whom I serve, stood by me this night,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, Fear not, Paul; thou must stand before Caesar; and behold,God has granted to thee all those that sail with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore be of good courage, men, for I believeGod that thus it shall be, as it has been said to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we must be cast ashore on a certain island.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the fourteenth night was come, we being driven about in Adria, towards the middle of the night the sailors supposed that some land neared them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having sounded found twenty fathoms, and having gone a little farther and having again sounded they found fifteen fathoms;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and fearing lest we should be cast on rocky places, casting four anchors out of the stern, they wished that day were come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the sailors wishing to flee out of the ship, and having let down the boat into the sea under pretext of being about to carry out anchors from the prow,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Paul said to the centurion and the soldiers, Unless these abide in the ship ye cannot be saved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the soldiers cut away the ropes of the boat and let her fall.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And while it was drawing on to daylight, Paul exhorted them all to partake of food, saying, Ye have passed the fourteenth day watching in expectation without taking food.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore I exhort you to partake of food, for this has to do with your safety; for not a hair from the head of any one of you shall perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, having said these things and taken a loaf, he gave thanks toGod before all, and having broken it began to eat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all taking courage, themselves also took food.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we were in the ship, all the souls, two hundred and seventy-six.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having satisfied themselves with food, they lightened the ship, casting out the wheat into the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when it was day they did not recognise the land; but they perceived a certain bay having a strand, on which they were minded, if they should be able, to run the ship ashore;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and, having cast off the anchors, they left [them] in the sea, at the same time loosening the lashings of the rudders, and hoisting the foresail to the wind, they made for the strand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And falling into a place where two seas met they ran the ship aground, and the prow having stuck itself fast remained unmoved, but the stern was broken by the force of the waves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [the] counsel of the soldiers was that they should kill the prisoners, lest any one should swim off and escape.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the centurion, desirous of saving Paul, hindered them of their purpose, and commanded those who were able to swim, casting themselves first [into the sea], to get out on land;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the rest, some on boards, some on some of the things [that came] from the ship; and thus it came to pass that all got safe to land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when we got safe [to land] we then knew that the island was called Melita.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the barbarians shewed us no common kindness; for, having kindled a fire, they took us all in because of the rain that was falling and because of the cold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Paul having gathered a [certain] quantity of sticks together in a bundle and laid [it] on the fire, a viper coming out from the heat seized his hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the barbarians saw the beast hanging from his hand, they said to one another, This man is certainly a murderer, whom, [though] saved out of the sea, Nemesis has not allowed to live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He however, having shaken off the beast into the fire, felt no harm.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they expected that he would have swollen or fallen down suddenly dead. But when they had expected a long time and saw nothing unusual happen to him, changing their opinion, they said he was agod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now in the country surrounding that place were the lands belonging to the chief man of the island, by name Publius, who received us and gave [us] hospitality three days in a very friendly way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it happened that the father of Publius lay ill of fever and dysentery; to whom Paul entered in, and having prayed and laid his hands on him cured him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But this having taken place, the rest also who had sicknesses in the island came and were healed:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who also honoured us with many honours, and on our leaving they made presents to us of what should minister to our wants.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after three months we sailed in a ship which had wintered in the island, an Alexandrian, with [the] Dioscuri for its ensign.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having come to Syracuse we remained three days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whence, going in a circuitous course, we arrived at Rhegium; and after one day, the wind having changed to south, on the second day we came to Puteoli,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: where, having found brethren, we were begged to stay with them seven days. And thus we went to Rome.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thence the brethren, having heard about us, came to meet us as far as Appii Forum and Tres Tabernae, whom when Paul saw, he thankedGod and took courage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when we came to Rome, [the centurion delivered up the prisoners to the praetorian prefect, but] Paul was allowed to remain by himself with the soldier who kept him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after three days, that he called together those who were the chief of the Jews; and when they had come together he said to them, Brethren, I having done nothing against the people or the customs of our forefathers, have been delivered a prisoner from Jerusalem into the hands of the Romans,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who having examined me were minded to let me go, because there was nothing worthy of death in me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the Jews speaking against it, I was compelled to appeal to Caesar, not as having anything to accuse my nation of.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this cause therefore I have called you to [me] to see and to speak to you; for on account of the hope of Israel I have this chain about me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to him, For our part, we have neither received letters from Judaea concerning thee, nor has any one of the brethren who has arrived reported or said anything evil concerning thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we beg to hear of thee what thou thinkest, for as concerning this sect it is known to us that it is everywhere spoken against.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having appointed him a day many came to him to the lodging, to whom he expounded, testifying of the kingdom ofGod, and persuading them concerning Jesus, both from the law of Moses and the prophets, from early morning to evening.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And some were persuaded of the things which were said, but some disbelieved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And being disagreed among themselves they left; Paul having spoken one word, Well spoke the Holy Spirit through Esaias the prophet to our fathers,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, Go to this people, and say, Hearing ye shall hear and not understand, and seeing ye shall see and not perceive.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the heart of this people has become fat, and they hear heavily with their ears, and they have closed their eyes; lest they should see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and understand with their heart, and be converted, and I should heal them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be it known to you therefore, that this salvation ofGod has been sent to the nations; they also will hear [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [And he having said this, the Jews went away, having great reasoning among themselves.]

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he remained two whole years in his own hired lodging, and received all who came to him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: preaching the kingdom ofGod, and teaching the things concerning the Lord Jesus Christ, with all freedom unhinderedly.
